body_txt: We've all seen Close Encounters of the Third Kind , The X-Files , and Buffy the Vampire Slayer . But what if these stories weren't false, but rather, made to distract people from the real aliens, government conspiracy and vampires? This trope describes the use of stories to serve as a psychological smoke screen. If someone came up and said that small grey aliens with big eyes just abducted him, you'd probably dismiss him as watching too much TV . It's used in media to show how big the conspiracy is. The people in charge know that they can't keep a lid on it all the time, so they start to release fictional accounts of their exploits, so if they ever do get seen, people will not believe the witnesses. Such a show is always popular , which isn't exactly reassuring about how much power the Conspiracy must have... There are two main variations: Sub-Trope of Cassandra Gambit . See also Plausible Deniability , Horror Disguised As Play , Literary Agent Hypothesis , Direct Line to the Author , and All Part of the Show . When the "fake fake show" is a direct part of the main plot, it's a Show Within a Show .
